findings.message: New metric app_frontend_raw_path_total emits one Prometheus series per raw full path (including the query string) via path="{p}" with no sanitization, no distinct-value cap, and no "other" overflow bucket. Raw paths are effectively unbounded, so this label has unbounded/high cardinality and will explode the Prometheus series count for this target. Every other label in this file (route, attribution, referrer) is sanitized and capped with an overflow bucket; this one breaks that contract.
findings.suggestion: Drop the query string and emit a sanitized, capped label. Reuse the existing pattern: strip the query string (path.split("?")[0]), pass through _san(...), and bump via self._bump_capped(self._raw_paths, sanitized, self.MAX_ROUTES) so excess distinct paths collapse into an "other" bucket. Better yet, drop this metric entirely since app_frontend_page_views_total already tracks views by bounded route.

findings.message: record_raw_path stores the raw full_path (including query string) as the dict key with no sanitization, no cap, and no overflow bucket, feeding the unbounded-cardinality app_frontend_raw_path_total series. The bounded-by-construction guarantee documented in the module docstring ("route and attribution labels are capped with an 'other' overflow bucket; all label values are sanitized; no free-text full URLs / query strings is ever stored") is violated here.
findings.suggestion: Sanitize and cap at ingest: route = _san(full_path.split("?")[0]) then self._bump_capped(self._raw_paths, route, self.MAX_ROUTES) inside the lock, matching record_page_view.
